paper.title: 591 high velocity stars in the Galactic halo selected from LAMOST DR7 and Gaia DR2

extraction.summary: Extraction covers only the 20 fastest HiVelSCs (Hivel1–Hivel20) for which detailed data are available in the paper's printed tables. The full catalog of 591 candidates is available only as an external CSV (http://paperdata.china-vo.org/LYB/lamostdr7_gaiadr2_hvs_591.csv) and is not included in the provided materials. The paper reports that at least 43 HiVelSCs have unbound probability >50% under the Watkins+2019 potential, and all 20 fastest fall in this group according to the printed escape probabilities. The remaining candidates cannot be extracted without the external file.

method_chain.summary: LAMOST DR7 low-resolution spectra (radial velocities, atmospheric parameters, spectral types) and Gaia DR2 astrometry and photometry.

source_refs.context: Description of LAMOST DR7 and Gaia DR2 data.

method_chain.summary: Cross-match LAMOST DR7 with Gaia DR2 using TOPCAT with a 5 arcsec radius.

Select stars with positive parallax and frac. parallax error <=0.2, keep highest S/N spectrum, select by V_GC>=450 or escaping in at least one potential, visually check RV, apply LASP RV zero-point correction, retain 591 HiVelSCs.
LAMOST Stellar Parameter Pipeline (LASP) radial velocities, zero-point corrected by -5.4 km/s using a catalog of RV standard stars.
Gaia DR2 astrometry: positions, proper motions, parallaxes, and correlation coefficients; applied quality criteria from Lindegren+2018 and Marchetti+2019 to define astrometric_flag.
Heliocentric distance from parallax inversion; Galactocentric coordinates via Monte Carlo sampling using the multivariate Gaussian astrometry.
Adopted solar Galactocentric distance R0=8.2 kpc, z0=25 pc, LSR circular velocity 238 km/s, solar peculiar velocity [U,V,W]=[14.0,12.24,7.25] km/s.
Compute Galactocentric Cartesian velocities Vx, Vy, Vz and total V_GC using 1000 MC realizations with the multivariate Gaussian astrometry and LAMOST RV.
Seven Galactic potential models: Watkins+2019, Gnedin+2005, Kenyon+2014, Koposov+2010, Paczynski+1990, MWPotential2014, Xue+2008.

parameters.name: potential_name
parameters.raw_value: Watkins+2019, Gnedin+2005, Kenyon+2014, Koposov+2010, Paczynski+1990, MWPotential2014, Xue+2008
parameters.value: Watkins+2019, Gnedin+2005, Kenyon+2014, Koposov+2010, Paczynski+1990, MWPotential2014, Xue+2008

method_chain.summary: Compute escape velocities at each star's position for all seven potentials; unbound probability Pub defined as fraction of MC realizations with V_GC > V_esc. A star is considered unbound if Pub >= 50% under a given model.

source_refs.context: Escape velocities and unbound probability definition.

method_chain.summary: Orbit integration backwards 10 Gyr using Galpy with MWPotential2014 (one of the seven potentials); 1000 MC realizations per star; derive eccentricity, Zmax, r_min, E-Phi(infinity).

Classification into HVS, HRS, RS, OUT based on thresholds on Pgc (r_min<1 kpc), PMW (r_min<25 kpc) and Pub. Criteria: HVS if Pgc>0.16; HRS if Pgc<0.16, PMW>0.5, Pub>0.5; RS if Pgc<0.16, PMW>0.5, Pub<0.5; OUT if Pgc<0.16, PMW<0.5.
astrometric_flag = 1 if star satisfies conservative Gaia astrometry quality criteria from Lindegren+2018 and Marchetti+2019; otherwise 0.
Atmospheric parameters (Teff, log g, [Fe/H]) from LASP and DD-Payne; alpha abundance from DD-Payne.

source_refs.context: DD-Payne method for atmospheric parameters and alpha abundance.

inclusion_assessment.summary: Among the 20 fastest HiVelSCs; unbound probability >50% under the conservative Watkins+2019 potential. The paper states at least 43 HiVelSCs are unbound, and this star is one of them as shown by Pub(W)=0.996.
inclusion_assessment.confidence_reason: Unbound status is directly tabulated and discussed in the text.

source_refs.context: At least 43 HiVelSCs have Pub > 50% being unbound in Watkins+2019.
Table A2 shows Hivel1 with Pub(W)=0.996, Origin_Class HRS, confirming unbound status.
Only 14 of the 591 HiVelSCs were reported in OFSC; others are new.
Table header with RA (deg) and Gaia provenance footnote d.
RA from Gaia DR2 implying ICRS at reference epoch J2016.0.
Table header with decl. (deg) and Gaia provenance footnote d.
decl. from Gaia DR2 implying ICRS at reference epoch J2016.0.

inclusion_assessment.summary: Among 20 fastest HiVelSCs; Pub(W)=0.995 > 0.5, unbound under Watkins+2019.

source_refs.context: At least 43 HiVelSCs unbound in Watkins+2019.
Table A2 shows Hivel2 with Pub(W)=0.995, confirmed unbound.

candidate_groups_considered.description: Full sample of 591 HiVelSCs from LAMOST DR7 and Gaia DR2. Only the 20 fastest are detailed in printed tables; the complete catalog is available externally at http://paperdata.china-vo.org/LYB/lamostdr7_gaiadr2_hvs_591.csv and could not be included.
candidate_groups_considered.reason: External catalog not available for extraction; only the 20 fastest with printed data are included.
